text:
In a quiet village tucked amongst rolling green hills, there lived an eccentric old inventor. His workshop was a mess of gears and scraps. On a crisp autumn day, he donned his goggles and swung open the door. A cold wind howled outside, and he chuckled heartily as he flicked a switch inside.

From the corner of his eye, he spotted something unusual—an apple that somehow had wings. He plucked it from a hook. "A flying apple?" he mused aloud. "Pity it wouldn't fly away. Maybe it's a magical relic." He rummaged through his jars and knick-knacks. "No magic I know," he decided.

That evening, beneath a waning crescent moon, the apples appeared once more. He reached for another. This one glowed. "Hmm," he thought. "This one sounds like... a dream?"

The apple buzzed. "Wake up!" it chirruped. He shook it. Out
